job_description:
Job Title: Entry level AI Engineer

Responsibilities:
- Build and set up machine learning models for Natural Language Processing (NLP).
- Work with AI models like BERT and GPT to handle different tasks.
- Optimize models to ensure they respond quickly and efficiently.
- Collaborate with the team to integrate AI into real applications.

Requirements:
- Comfortable writing code in Python.
- Familiarity with AI tools like PyTorch or Hugging Face.
- Basic understanding of how computers process text (Arabic AI/AraBERT is a plus).
- Ability to write clean and organized code.

candidate_cv.career_overview: Analyst with extensive experience in Information Technology. Proficiencies include trouble shooting hardware and software issues. Experienced Service tech with excellent client and project management skills. Action-oriented with strong ability to communicate effectively with technology, executive, and business audiences.

responsibilities: Monitored multiple databases to keep track of all company inventory.
Successfully led key projects which resulted in [positive outcome].
Provided onsite training.
Provided Tier1 & Tier2 level support.
Perform Tier 3 level support to customers on operational or maintenance aspects of system equipment.
Perform service, repair and/or install of company products including system hardware, software, PCs and POS equipment.
Perform general maintenance, repair computer systems and peripheral equipment.
Replace and install cable (cat5) network cable.
Managed and trouble shoot telecom phone system.
Level 1 Field Service Tech Provided Tierl & Tier2 level support.
Perform Tier 3 level support to customers on operational or maintenance aspects of system equipment.
Perform service, repair and/or install of company products including system hardware, software, PCs and POS equipment.
Perform general maintenance, repair computer systems and peripheral equipment.
Replace and install cable (cat5) network cable.
Provided hardware support related to pc, lap tops, note books technical issues, software application and OS issues.
Assists clients with recommending, scheduling and implementing system hardware and/or software upgrades based on needs and anticipated growth.
Set up and maintained active directory requests, setup network ids/email accounts and remote desktop support for specific client.
Offered assist for website design, layout and hosting.
Offered residential customers technical support with hardware and software issues related to hard drives, power supply replacements and virus removal.
Conducted analysis to address network issues which led to install of a T1 line.
Monitored multiple databases to keep track of all company technology inventory.
Provide trouble shooting support in matters related to computer hardware and software issues.
Identify any computer architectural requirements; establish and upgrade systems; maintain installation records; improve system performance; maintain technical knowledge.
Set up and maintain local area network using cable layout and fiber optic connections.
Configure and trouble shooting routers, pix, Domino Server and Microsoft Windows 2003 Server.
Oversee staff related issues related to computer upgrades, hardware problems, virus, email accounts/network ids, remote desktop support and training.
Develop policies and procedures for internet access and antivirus installations.
Communicate effectively with end users, vendors and upper management.
Trained end users in office applications and security issues.
Managed all pc hardware and software office applications and installations at the local branch offices, feed mills and truck shop.

scorecard.reason: The candidate provided vague and hypothetical answers for most of the questions, lacking specific evidence and proactive traits. [cite: 13]
detailed_report: The candidate's responses lacked depth and context in their technical explanations. [cite: 13]
They failed to provide concrete examples of their work with Python, Linux, or cognitive flexibility. [cite: 13]
They frequently used the phrase 'I didn't have an answer for that' and did not engage in the STAR method. [cite: 13]
Answers were often incomplete and did not demonstrate a strong understanding of required skills. [cite: 13]
Responses lacked ownership and showed a lack of methodical and proactive behavior.
The candidate did not ask any questions during the interview. [cite: 14]
